pool.baseline:
hey, i just got this nda from a potential partner and i dont really want to pay a lawyer just to read it yet. can u just give me a quick plain english summary of the main parts? like what im actually agreeing to and how long it lasts. 

here is the text:

"CONFIDENTIALITY AND NON-DISCLOSURE AGREEMENT

This Non-Disclosure Agreement (the "Agreement") is entered into as of October 12, 2023, by and between Zephyr Solutions Group ("Disclosing Party") and Arclight Manufacturing LLC ("Recipient").

1. DEFINITION OF CONFIDENTIAL INFORMATION. "Confidential Information" shall mean any and all non-public, proprietary, or sensitive information, whether oral, written, or electronic, disclosed by the Disclosing Party to the Recipient, including but not limited to business plans, client lists, technical specifications, and trade secrets.

2. OBLIGATIONS OF RECIPIENT. The Recipient agrees to hold all Confidential Information in strict confidence and shall exercise the same degree of care to prevent unauthorized disclosure as it uses to protect its own confidential information of a similar nature, but in no event less than a reasonable degree of care. Recipient shall not use the Confidential Information for any purpose other than to evaluate a potential business relationship between the parties.

3. TERM AND TERMINATION. This Agreement shall commence on the Effective Date and shall continue for a period of three (3) years. The obligations of confidentiality with respect to trade secrets shall survive the termination of this Agreement for as long as such information remains a trade secret under applicable law.

4. REMEDIES. The Recipient acknowledges that any breach of this Agreement may cause irreparable harm to the Disclosing Party for which monetary damages may be inadequate, and therefore the Disclosing Party shall be entitled to seek injunctive relief in addition to any other remedies available at law."
